metadata.title: Breeder profile web research - July 15 batch 8 P
metadata.notes: Public-web research only; no external communication, accounts, forms, or outreach were used. Breeder, inventor, employer, applicant, licensee, agent, and legal rights-holder roles remain distinct. Candidate parents are proposed only where the cited sources support an institutional, corporate, or succession relationship; cultivar-level title evidence still controls. Malformed and mixed registry names are flagged for legacy review or record splitting rather than force-matched.

profiles.ownershipType: unresolved generic registry-name fragment spanning multiple legal entities
profiles.ownershipSummary: The exact dashboard name is not a defensible standalone company identity. Underlying records containing this fragment resolve to multiple unrelated nursery businesses, including Fall Creek Farm and Nursery, Inc., Lassen Canyon Nursery, Inc., and The Burchell Nursery, Inc. These businesses have different ownership, crops, locations, and breeding programs and must not be aggregated.
profiles.headquarters: Unresolved; mixed records include businesses in Oregon and California, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: No leadership can be assigned to this generic fragment. Each record must first be restored to its complete applicant, holder, or assignee name and then researched under that entity's own profile.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: No single parent is supportable because the profile combines unrelated companies.

webResearchSources.label: Fall Creek official company history
Lassen Canyon Nursery official website
Burchell Breeding official website

profiles.webResearchNotes: Mandatory split: suppress this aggregate from scoring and search results. Reassign every raw row to the complete source name, with registry evidence retained. The example cultivars illustrate the mixed population and do not belong to one portfolio.

profiles.ownershipType: retired public-university strawberry breeder and former private-company cofounder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Larson was a University of California, Davis strawberry breeder and pomologist, not the personal owner of every cultivar naming him. After retirement he co-founded California Berry Cultivars with Douglas Shaw. UC sources distinguish UC-developed germplasm and varieties from later CBC interests, so the dashboard must preserve the legal holder shown for each cultivar rather than roll all Larson records to either organization.
profiles.headquarters: Historical affiliation: University of California, Davis, California, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: Larson joined UC Davis in 1991 and later retired. UC Davis now operates the continuing public strawberry breeding and licensing program through an institutional team. California Berry Cultivars is a separate post-retirement company and must be treated separately where it appears in title records.
profiles.candidateParent: The Regents of the University of California
profiles.candidateParentBasis: High-confidence institutional rollup for UC-developed cultivars only. UC's history and licensing pages support the affiliation, while the settlement background requires separate treatment of California Berry Cultivars interests.

webResearchSources.label: UC Davis strawberry breeding historical timeline
UC Davis litigation and ownership background
UC Davis current strawberry licensing program

profiles.webResearchNotes: Mandatory split by cultivar and filing: UC records roll to The Regents; CBC records remain with CBC or the holder proven by assignment evidence. Do not treat Larson's breeder status as personal ownership. Suppress the individual as an acquisition target while retaining him in breeder and succession analysis.

profiles.canonicalName: Timothy P. Sheehan, Sheehan Genetics LLC
profiles.ownershipType: deceased breeder combined with a legacy private breeding company and later corporate successors
profiles.ownershipSummary: The exact profile improperly combines Timothy P. Sheehan, who died in 2009, with Sheehan Genetics LLC. SNFL's official history states that it acquired Sheehan Genetics and became its sole owner. Following the SNFL-IFG combination, cultivar-level assignment records show certain Sheegene rights moving to Bloom Fresh entities. That history supports a successor rollup, but not a blanket transfer of every right in every jurisdiction without title evidence.
profiles.headquarters: Historical company operations: Fresno, California, United States; current global successor: Bloom Fresh
profiles.leadershipSummary: Sheehan's breeding legacy continued through SNFL's North American program. SNFL and International Fruit Genetics later combined under the Bloom Fresh identity. Current commercial and breeding responsibility should be researched at Bloom Fresh, while historical filings must retain Sheehan Genetics and SNFL where those were the recorded holders.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: SNFL's official history establishes acquisition of Sheehan Genetics, and patent assignment records establish Bloom Fresh ownership for reviewed Sheegene titles. Apply the rollup only where the applicable title or assignment record confirms it.

webResearchSources.label: SNFL official company history
SNFL and IFG combination into Bloom Fresh
Sheegene 28 patent assignment history

profiles.webResearchNotes: Mandatory split into the deceased breeder, legacy company, and cultivar-level legal holder. Preserve Sheehan as breeder, Sheehan Genetics as historical applicant/assignee, SNFL as intermediate owner, and Bloom Fresh only where a title or assignment supports the current rollup. This is part of a large strategic platform, not a standalone small-company target.

profiles.ownershipType: malformed legacy company name with unresolved current identity
profiles.ownershipSummary: The dashboard string contains corrupted quotation marks. An official Georgian plant-variety register resolves the historical name as VANRIK GREEN ENERGY LTD and shows the reviewed blueberry registrations as declared null. A later public accelerator report mentions a Vanrik Agro Group blueberry project in Kazakhstan, but no primary source reviewed establishes that it is the same legal entity or successor.
profiles.headquarters: Georgia in the legacy registry; possible Kazakhstan activity is unverified
profiles.leadershipSummary: Current leadership and legal status could not be verified from durable primary sources. The exact Georgian registrations reviewed were terminated or declared null, and the listed blueberry cultivars are established cultivars rather than evidence of a proprietary VANRIK breeding portfolio.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Possible name and crop continuity only. No reviewed primary source proves legal continuity between VANRIK GREEN ENERGY LTD and Vanrik Agro Group.

webResearchSources.label: Sakpatenti official registered-varieties status list
PFAN annual report mentioning Vanrik Agro Group

profiles.webResearchNotes: Keep unresolved and exclude from acquisition scoring. Normalize the mojibake to VANRIK GREEN ENERGY LTD only as a display correction, not as a current-company match. Do not count the null Georgian registrations as active proprietary IP and do not merge Vanrik Agro Group without legal-identity evidence.

profiles.ownershipType: former private-company executive, shareholder, and named breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Pierron-Darbonne is the former Planasa chairman and chief executive associated with many Planasa cultivars, but breeder status does not establish personal ownership. Official registry evidence identifies Planasa as the owner of reviewed varieties and Pierron-Darbonne as an initial breeder. He was replaced in Planasa governance in 2020, and Cinven and Label Investments sold Planasa to EW Group in 2023.
profiles.headquarters: Historical operating affiliation: Planasa, Valtierra, Navarra, Spain
profiles.leadershipSummary: Pierron-Darbonne is no longer Planasa's operating leader or owner. Planasa identifies Michael Brinkmann as chief executive, and the company is now owned by EW Group. Records associated with Darbonne Pépinière or other historical entities require separate treatment from Planasa-held rights.
profiles.candidateParent: Planasa, now owned by EW Group
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official registry material supports Planasa ownership for reviewed cultivars, while the 2023 transaction announcement establishes EW Group as Planasa's current owner. This does not cover non-Planasa records.

webResearchSources.label: Planasa official history and leadership
Cinven sale of Planasa to EW Group
Swiss federal variety bulletin identifying owner and breeder roles

profiles.webResearchNotes: Mandatory split by legal holder. Roll Planasa-owned records to Planasa/EW Group, preserve Pierron-Darbonne as breeder and former leader, and keep Darbonne Pépinière or personal legacy records separate unless an assignment proves consolidation. The person is not a current standalone acquisition target.

profiles.ownershipType: Uruguayan public non-state agricultural research institute
profiles.ownershipSummary: The exact legal name resolves to Uruguay's Instituto Nacional de Investigación Agropecuaria. INIA is a public non-state research institution with national fruit, vegetable, citrus, and strawberry breeding programs. It is an institutional breeder and rights manager, not a private acquisition target.
profiles.leadershipSummary: INIA's official authorities page identifies Gerardo Marchesini as Director Nacional. The institute operates through multiple research stations and program teams, providing durable institutional continuity rather than dependence on a single breeder.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: INIA is a standalone Uruguayan public non-state institute; no corporate parent is applicable.

webResearchSources.label: INIA official institutional description
INIA official authorities
INIA intensive plant production and breeding program

profiles.webResearchNotes: Keep INIA Uruguay distinct from similarly abbreviated institutions in Spain and other countries. Country of filing is not evidence of institutional identity. Preserve named breeders and territorial holders at record level, but suppress the institution as an acquisition candidate.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Castagnoli was a breeder associated with Consorzio Italiano Vivaisti (CIV). Patent records for reviewed CIV cultivars identify CIV as assignee, while later registry material identifies Castagnoli's heirs in the breeder chain. His name and spelling variants should be consolidated as one deceased breeder, not treated as a current personal rights holder.
profiles.headquarters: Historical affiliation: CIV, San Giuseppe di Comacchio, Ferrara, Italy
profiles.leadershipSummary: Current breeding and intellectual-property management continue institutionally through CIV. Succession evidence includes heirs named in registry material, but that does not make the heirs the holder of every CIV right. Current commercial inquiries should be directed to CIV.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Reviewed patent records identify CIV as assignee and official registry material links Castagnoli and his heirs to CIV cultivars. Apply the rollup only to CIV-supported records.

webResearchSources.label: CIV official website
CIVNI patent and assignment record
Sakpatenti official registry naming CIV and Castagnoli heir

profiles.webResearchNotes: Consolidate Gianfranco Castagnoli spelling and deceased-name variants into one breeder identity. Roll reviewed CIV-assigned records to CIV, preserve heir references as succession-chain evidence, and verify any non-CIV title independently. Suppress the individual as a direct acquisition target.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Trapero Ramírez is a current University of Córdoba professor and UCOLIVO breeder. He is a named breeder of Todolivo I-series olives and UCO-associated cultivars, but the legal holder varies: reviewed I-series filings identify Todolivo as applicant or holder, while UCOLIVO identifies the University of Córdoba for Sultana. His breeder status is not personal ownership.
profiles.leadershipSummary: The official UCOLIVO profile lists Trapero Ramírez as Profesor Permanente Laboral working on olive and pistachio breeding. The program includes a multi-person institutional bench, reducing single-person succession risk.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: University of Córdoba olive breeding program
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official employment and program sources establish the institutional affiliation. They do not establish UCO ownership for I-series records held or applied for by Todolivo.

webResearchSources.label: UCOLIVO official breeding team profile
UCOLIVO official cultivar and title overview
Official I-20 patent application naming Todolivo and breeders

profiles.webResearchNotes: Mandatory cultivar-level split: preserve Trapero Ramírez as breeder; roll I-series rights to Todolivo only where registry or patent evidence supports it; retain UCO as the holder for Sultana where the official program states that title. Suppress the individual as an acquisition target.

profiles.ownershipType: private multigenerational nursery and breeding company
profiles.ownershipSummary: The exact dashboard name should normalize to Viveros Provedo, S.A. The company states that it was founded in 1926 and is now led by the third generation. It operates nursery, laboratory, R&D, grape, stone-fruit, and other woody-plant programs. Spanish official records identify Viveros Provedo as a title holder and, for some grape work, a co-applicant with the University of La Rioja.
profiles.headquarters: C/ Canicalejo s/n, 26006 Logroño, La Rioja, Spain
profiles.leadershipSummary: Brothers Javier and Ignacio Provedo took over management in 2003, providing documented family succession. The company reports substantial propagation scale, including more than four million grape plants annually, and an internal breeding and laboratory platform.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official company and Spanish registry sources support normalization of the reversed dashboard name to the operating legal entity. No external corporate parent was identified.

webResearchSources.label: Viveros Provedo official company history and contact
Spanish MAPA official entity record
Spanish MAPA official variety-title results

profiles.webResearchNotes: Normalize the name but verify holder and co-owner at cultivar level, especially joint University of La Rioja records. This is a credible private breeding platform with family succession, but its reported propagation scale and large observed portfolio suggest it may exceed the target size range; retain as a strategic benchmark until financial size is verified.

profiles.ownershipType: independent private family-owned blueberry breeding, nursery, farming, and marketing group
profiles.ownershipSummary: Mountain Blue Orchards is a family-owned Australian blueberry platform founded by Ridley Bell. It combines breeding, commercial orchards, nursery propagation, and global cultivar licensing. Reviewed U.S. patent evidence assigns current cultivar rights directly to Mountain Blue Orchards Pty Ltd.
profiles.headquarters: New South Wales, Australia; postal contact PO Box 6001, South Lismore NSW 2480
profiles.leadershipSummary: The official history states that Andrew Bell now oversees the company while founder Ridley Bell continues to lead R&D. That father-to-son operating transition is a clear, source-backed succession structure rather than a sole-breeder dependency.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official sources describe the business as family owned and identify its operating group entities; no external corporate parent was identified.

webResearchSources.label: Mountain Blue official history and succession
Mountain Blue official group entities and contact
Ridley7301 patent and assignee record

profiles.webResearchNotes: Keep Mountain Blue distinct from BerryWorld and other marketing or licensing partners; partnership is not ownership. The documented family succession and vertically integrated platform are relevant, but more than 200 hectares and the company's claim to Australia's largest commercial blueberry nursery indicate likely strategic scale above the target range.

profiles.ownershipType: nonprofit university direct-support organization and intellectual-property manager
profiles.ownershipSummary: Florida Foundation Seed Producers, Inc. is a 501(c)(3) direct-support organization of the University of Florida. It manages U.S. plant patents, plant variety protection, international plant breeders' rights, trademarks, licensing, and royalty collection for UF/IFAS cultivars. It is an IP commercialization entity, not the breeder of every record attributed to it.
profiles.leadershipSummary: FFSP operates a continuing technology-transfer office and licensing process supported by the University of Florida agricultural research system. Royalties are reinvested in UF/FAES breeding programs, providing institutional continuity rather than private-company succession exposure.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: FFSP's official description establishes it as a distinct nonprofit direct-support organization serving the University of Florida. The relationship is institutional, not a conventional parent-subsidiary ownership claim.

webResearchSources.label: FFSP official organization and IP role
FFSP official technology-transfer facilities
FFSP official licensing process

profiles.webResearchNotes: Correct dashboard role labels: FFSP is generally the IP manager, licensor, applicant, or holder for UF/IFAS work, while named UF faculty and teams are the breeders. Preserve licensees and commercial partners separately. Suppress FFSP as a private acquisition target but retain it as an important rights-management counterparty.

profiles.ownershipType: private-company founder, president, and named olive breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Gómez Porras is the founder and president of Todolivo and a named breeder of the Todolivo I-series developed with the University of Córdoba team. Official patent and registry evidence identifies Todolivo S.L., rather than Gómez Porras personally, as applicant or title holder for reviewed I-series cultivars.
profiles.headquarters: Todolivo, C/ Ingeniero Torroja y Miret 22, Córdoba, Spain
profiles.leadershipSummary: A 2025 Todolivo announcement identifies Gómez Porras as president and states that his son, Juan María Gómez Romero, a partner-director, represented the company when Gómez Porras could not attend for health reasons. This is a meaningful family succession signal, but it does not by itself prove a formal leadership or ownership transfer.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official patent and variety-register evidence identifies Todolivo as applicant or title holder for reviewed I-series records; Gómez Porras remains a named breeder and founder.

webResearchSources.label: Official I-20 patent application naming Todolivo and breeders
Sakpatenti I-15 bulletin identifying Todolivo as title holder
Todolivo 2025 leadership and family succession signal

profiles.webResearchNotes: Roll reviewed I-series rights to Todolivo, preserve Gómez Porras and the UCO team as breeders, and verify territorial title record by record. This is a potentially relevant private, founder-led sourcing profile with an observable family bench; financial scale, ownership, and willingness to transact remain unverified.

profiles.ownershipType: public-university professor emeritus, olive breeder, and private-company cofounder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Barranco Navero is a University of Córdoba professor emeritus and a prominent olive breeder. His records span distinct programs and title structures: UCO/IFAPA cultivars such as Sikitita and Martina, UCO-associated Sultana, and Todolivo I-series cultivars for which reviewed filings identify Todolivo as applicant or holder. His official UCO biography also identifies him as a cofounder of Pomología S.L.; that company must not be assumed to own unrelated rights.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Barranco Navero is emeritus. Current UCOLIVO work is carried by a broader University of Córdoba team, including active faculty and commercial collaborators. That bench reduces dependence on the historical lead breeder, although ownership and commercial control remain cultivar specific.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: University of Córdoba olive breeding program
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official biography and program pages establish the institutional affiliation, but records involving IFAPA, Todolivo, or Pomología require their own title evidence.

webResearchSources.label: University of Córdoba official Diego Barranco profile
UCOLIVO official cultivar and title overview
Official I-20 patent application naming Todolivo and breeders

profiles.webResearchNotes: Mandatory split by cultivar and holder: UCO/IFAPA, UCO, Todolivo, and any Pomología records remain distinct. Preserve Barranco Navero as breeder and emeritus program founder; do not infer personal or Pomología ownership from authorship or affiliation.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Fischer led apple breeding at Dresden-Pillnitz from 1978 and retired in 2002. Historical publications credit her with the Pi and Re cultivar series, but reviewed patent evidence names the federal breeding institute, not Fischer personally, as assignee. The current institutional successor is the Julius Kühn Institute's Institute for Breeding Research on Fruit Crops.
profiles.headquarters: Historical affiliation: Dresden-Pillnitz, Germany; current successor institute: Dresden, Germany
profiles.leadershipSummary: Fischer retired in 2002 and was succeeded in the apple program by Andreas Peil. The current JKI fruit-breeding institute is led by Prof. Henryk Flachowsky and maintains an active institutional breeding bench.
profiles.primaryContactName: JKI Institute for Breeding Research on Fruit Crops
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Current institutional successor and breeding contact
profiles.candidateParent: Julius Kühn Institute and its predecessor federal breeding institutions
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official institutional history and patent records support a federal-institute lineage. Historical assignee names must be preserved rather than rewritten as if JKI held every right at original filing.

webResearchSources.label: Official Pillnitz horticulture history and Fischer succession
JKI current fruit breeding institute
Rebella patent and historical federal assignee

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress the retired individual as an acquisition target. Consolidate name variants under Fischer as breeder, but preserve the exact historical institute or assignee on each filing. Use JKI as the current institutional successor and contact, not as an automatic retroactive owner.

profiles.ownershipType: public-university olive breeder and tenured professor
profiles.ownershipSummary: Muñoz Díez is a current University of Córdoba professor and UCOLIVO breeder. She is named on Todolivo I-series records and UCO-associated cultivars, but reviewed evidence assigns I-series applicant or holder roles to Todolivo and identifies different title structures for Sikitita 2 and Sultana. Her breeder status is not personal ownership.
profiles.leadershipSummary: The official University of Córdoba profile identifies Muñoz Díez as Profesora Titular de Universidad. She works within the multi-person UCOLIVO breeding group, providing an active institutional bench alongside other current faculty and commercial collaborators.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: University of Córdoba olive breeding program
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official employment and program sources establish her institutional affiliation. They do not establish UCO ownership for all Todolivo or joint UCO/IFAPA records.

webResearchSources.label: University of Córdoba official Concepción Muñoz Díez profile
UCOLIVO official cultivar and title overview
Official I-20 patent application naming Todolivo and breeders

profiles.webResearchNotes: Mandatory split by cultivar and legal holder: Todolivo I-series, UCO/IFAPA Sikitita 2, and UCO-associated Sultana must remain distinct. Preserve Muñoz Díez as breeder and current faculty; do not infer personal ownership or merge all records to one company.
